Project overview

Project overview

17-marker panel reveals 10 cell phenotypes across 79 samples, 58561942 cells

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 300 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. 
•Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 14 RCNs
P .S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency

Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in diﬀerent clinical groupsB-cell, Stromal-CAF and Stroma-residence immune niches are enriched in HRP group.

UTAG reveals ﬁne-grained domains with per-sample heterogeneity
Radius: 60px ≈ 20um 
(the value in the paper)
Leiden clustering
Resolution: 0.2
Over-clustered!

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: 
Domain * cell type%
Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: 
Domain * cell type%
Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Tissue domains are continuum http://www.statistik.lmu.de/~eugster/publications/ talk-2012-HIIT-archetypes.pdf

Project overview

Take home message: •t-CyCIF images are the ground truth, provide us information about cellular identity, proportion, and tissue architecture. Better spatial statistics methods should be applied. 
•t-CyCIF -> ROIs selection -> build TMA -> Xenium ->“What are cells doing here? What genes are expressing, how do they interact…”
•WES and bulk RNA-seq can provide us patient-level information / quantify heterogeneity.

Thanks for listening!
